missing_info: Provide the exact timestamp (UTC) when the alert was observed for R2 Serial0/1/1 and R3 Serial0/1/1 (to correlate with collected probes).

Collect current, real-time interface status for both devices (output of: show interfaces Serial0/1/1) — include admin state, line-protocol/oper state, last input/last output, counters (input/output errors, CRC), and time since last state change.

Provide the current running-config for the interfaces (output of: show running-config interface Serial0/1/1) on R2 and R3 to confirm whether the interface has a configured shutdown or other interface-level commands that would force admin down.

Provide controller-level details for the serial interfaces on both routers (output of: show controllers serial 0/1/1) to check DCE/DTE, clock rate and physical layer status, plus any relevant syslog/messages around the alert time showing link up/down events.

data.intent_description: Investigate interfaces Serial0/1/1 on R2 (oper down: lower-layer-down) and R3 (oper down, admin down) to determine root cause and required remediation.

missing_info: Provide the exact timestamp (UTC) when the alert was observed for R2 Serial0/1/1 and R3 Serial0/1/1 (to correlate with collected probes).

Collect current, real-time interface status for both devices (output of: show interfaces Serial0/1/1) — include admin state, line-protocol/oper state, last input/last output, counters (input/output errors, CRC), and time since last state change.

Provide the current running-config for the interfaces (output of: show running-config interface Serial0/1/1) on R2 and R3 to confirm whether the interface has a configured shutdown or other interface-level commands that would force admin down.

data.intent_description: R2 Serial0/1/1 transitioned to oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up (likely physical-layer/DCE-DTE or line issue). R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down causing oper down. Impact: both serial links are down; restore R3 interface administratively and collect diagnostics to determine/fix R2 physical failure.

plan.problem: R2 Serial0/1/1: oper=lower-layer-down, admin=up (physical/clocking/remote-side issue). R3 Serial0/1/1: admin=down causing oper=down (interface shutdown). Missing info: exact UTC timestamps of alerts for both interfaces; live 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' outputs (admin state, line-protocol, last input/last output, counters, time since last change) for both routers; 'show running-config interface Serial0/1/1' for both; 'show controllers serial 0/1/1' for both; relevant syslog/messages around the alert times.
plan.fix_summary: 1) Enable R3 Serial0/1/1 (remove shutdown). 2) Collect detailed interface, running-config, controller, and syslog data for R2 and R3 to diagnose R2 physical-layer fault.

messages.content:
{
  "intent": "check_and_fix",
  "intent_description": "R2 Serial0/1/1 transitioned to oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up (likely physical-layer/DCE-DTE or line issue). R3 Serial0/1/1 is administratively down causing oper down. Impact: both serial links are down; restore R3 interface administratively and collect diagnostics to determine/fix R2 physical failure.",
  "target": null,
  "needs_fix": true,
  "plan": {
    "problem": "R2 Serial0/1/1: oper=lower-layer-down, admin=up (physical/clocking/remote-side issue). R3 Serial0/1/1: admin=down causing oper=down (interface shutdown). Missing info: exact UTC timestamps of alerts for both interfaces; live 'show interfaces Serial0/1/1' outputs (admin state, line-protocol, last input/last output, counters, time since last change) for both routers; 'show running-config interface Serial0/1/1' for both; 'show controllers serial 0/1/1' for both; relevant syslog/messages around the alert times.",
    "fix_summary": "1) Enable R3 Serial0/1/1 (remove shutdown). 2) Collect detailed interface, running-config, controller, and syslog data for R2 and R3 to diagnose R2 physical-layer fault.",
    "plan_steps": [
      {
        "id": 1,
        "device": "router2",
        "action": "collect_interface_status",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": [
          "include=admin_state",
          "include=line_protocol",
          "include=last_input",
          "include=last_output",
          "include=input_errors",
          "include=output_errors",
          "include=crc_errors",
          "include=time_since_last_change"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 2,
        "device": "router2",
        "action": "collect_running_config",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": []
      },
      {
        "id": 3,
        "device": "router2",
        "action": "collect_controller_info",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": [
          "include=DCE_DTE",
          "include=clock_rate",
          "include=physical_layer_status"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 4,
        "device": "router2",
        "action": "collect_syslog",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": [
          "time_window=last_30_minutes",
          "include=link_up_down_events"
        ]
      },
      {
        "id": 5,
        "device": "router3",
        "action": "collect_running_config",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": []
      },
      {
        "id": 6,
        "device": "router3",
        "action": "set_interface_admin_up",
        "target": "Serial0/1/1",
        "parameters": []
      }
    ]
  }
}

messages.content:
What was observed
- Initial alerts: R2 Serial0/1/1 reported oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up; R3 Serial0/1/1 reported admin=down → link between R2↔R3 was down.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Most likely a transient physical/clocking / lower-layer issue on the R2↔R3 serial leg. R3 was reported admin-down by the alerting system, but running-config shows no "shutdown" (so administrative disable was not present).

Changes made
- Collected live interface status and running-config from R2 and R3 for Serial0/1/1.
- Attempted to set R3 Serial0/1/1 administratively up (command run; interface already enabled).

Verification — passed
- R2 Serial0/1/1 now: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, last_change=2026-05-27T14:55:52Z, input_errors=0, output_errors=0.
- R3 Serial0/1/1 now: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, last_change=2026-05-27T14:13:53Z, input_errors=0, output_errors=0.
- Running-configs on both routers show the Serial0/1/1 stanzas and no "shutdown".
- No remaining issues reported.

If you want, I can:
- Pull controller-level details and recent syslog (link up/down events and DCE/DTE/clocking) to confirm root-cause and rule out recurring hardware/clock issues.

data.last_message:
What was observed
- Initial alerts: R2 Serial0/1/1 reported oper=lower-layer-down while admin=up; R3 Serial0/1/1 reported admin=down → link between R2↔R3 was down.

Diagnosis (main cause)
- Most likely a transient physical/clocking / lower-layer issue on the R2↔R3 serial leg. R3 was reported admin-down by the alerting system, but running-config shows no "shutdown" (so administrative disable was not present).

Changes made
- Collected live interface status and running-config from R2 and R3 for Serial0/1/1.
- Attempted to set R3 Serial0/1/1 administratively up (command run; interface already enabled).

Verification — passed
- R2 Serial0/1/1 now: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, last_change=2026-05-27T14:55:52Z, input_errors=0, output_errors=0.
- R3 Serial0/1/1 now: admin_state=up, oper_state=up, last_change=2026-05-27T14:13:53Z, input_errors=0, output_errors=0.
- Running-configs on both routers show the Serial0/1/1 stanzas and no "shutdown".
- No remaining issues reported.

If you want, I can:
- Pull controller-level details and recent syslog (link up/down events and DCE/DTE/clocking) to confirm root-cause and rule out recurring hardware/clock issues.
